entry.title: Re: how to add and populate two custom fields in addition...
entry.content:
Hello @MuditJain 

You need to use the ME_GUI_PO_CUST BAdI enhancement:

The Business Add-In (BAdI) ME_GUI_PO_CUST enables you to integrate your own subscreens into the Enjoy purchase order at both header and item levels.

Check the BAdI documentation and the example implementation class CL_EXM_IM_ME_GUI_PO_CUST.

There numerous articles on how to use the enhancement, e.g.

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/how-to-create-customer-screen-on-me21n-me22n-me23n-item-level-using-badi/ba-p/13263743
  • https://www.scribd.com/document/520632205/ME21N-ME22N-ME23N-screen-enhancement-BADI-ME-GUI-PO-CUST-Programmer-Sought

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Fast-Track Your AI Journey with SAP HANA Cloud’s Vector Engine Through Our New Expert Guided service
entry.content:
Fast-Track Your AI Journey with Hands-On Experience of SAP HANA Cloud’s Vector Engine Through Our New Expert-Guided Implementation service

Are you ready to put generative AI to work using your own enterprise data - in a secure and scalable way?

SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council is piloting a new expert guided implementation service (EGI) focused on multi-model programming with SAP HANA Cloud vector engine and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). As a participant, you’ll gain exclusive early access to content, influence the final offering with your feedback, and build hands-on experience before general availability. This isn’t just another webinar - it’s a practical, step-by-step experience where you’ll configure, test, and build solutions in your own environment, with guidance from an expert throughout.

This structured approach ensures you gain both theoretical knowledge and practical experience, helping you build confidence in using SAP solutions effectively. Since SAP Enterprise Support is included with every SAP cloud subscription, you can access this offering at no additional cost. 

After the completion of the 5 day remote workshop, you will have the knowledge of multiple model programming, including scenarios using spatial and graph processing, AutoML, vector engine and document store and the knowledge to query a document store through generative AI.


What You’ll Gain
 This service will equip you with the skills to leverage:

  • Spatial and graph data processing
  • AutoML and document stores
  • RAG application that connects enterprise data to LLMs
  • Vector search in SAP HANA Cloud

Why It Matters?
With the vector engine, SAP HANA Cloud becomes the bedrock of enterprise-grade AI applications - supporting similarity search, contextual insights, and intelligent applications all from within your secure SAP landscape.

What You’ll Do?

  • Set up and develop in a HANA Cloud trial or productive account
  • Work directly with spatial, graph, and vector data models
  • Extend a real RAG scenario using your own development environment
  • Gain practical experience with SAP expert support

Who Should Join?
The service is tailor-made for:

  • Developers and Data Scientists building intelligent apps
  • Process consultants driving innovation
  • Modelers exploring new use cases in SAP HANA Cloud

Join the EGI service and gain first-hand experience with SAP’s latest innovation in multi-model programming. Learn more and register for the service by contacting SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council through esac@sap.com

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 

About the author: 

  • Robert Kelly is Architect Lead for BTP for SAP Enterprise Support. Based in Dublin, Ireland, Robert has been with SAP since 2005, holding various roles across the organization. Today, he focuses on helping businesses implement their digital transformation through SAP Enterprise Support offerings.

entry.title: HANA - Log /hana/log full situation
entry.content:
If HANA is running into a Disk Full Situation, mostly in a replication setup, it is the /hana/log volume filling up with Log Segments.

To get the actual Log Segment Situation and partial immediate help, either use:

Option A, via SQL statement:

select b.host, b.service_name, a.state, count(*) from "PUBLIC"."M_LOG_SEGMENTS" a join "PUBLIC"."M_SERVICES" b on (a.host = b.host AND a.port = b.port) group by b.host, b.service_name, a.state;

Option B, via OS command as <sid>adm user:

hdbnsutil -listServices --allDatabases --sapcontrol=1 | grep -P "service_name|process_id|volume|database_name" | awk '/^service.*service_name=/ {s=$0; next} /^service.*process_id=/ {p=$0; next} /^service.*volume=/ {split($0,v,"="); if (v[2] != 0) {print s; print p; print $0; show_dbname=1} else {show_dbname=0}; next} /^service.*database_name=/ {if (show_dbname) {print $0; print ""}}'; for i in $(find -L /hana/log/${DIR_INSTANCE:9:3} -name 'hdb*' -type d) ; do echo $i ; hdblogdiag seglist $i | grep -oP 'Formatting|Preallocated|Writing|\bClosed\b|Truncated|BackedUp|RetainedFree|\bFree\b|Unknown|ClosedIncomplete|FreeIncomplete' | sort | uniq -c ; done

A Sample of Option B:

Sample Setup:
physical Hostname: hdb-11-85static
virtual Hosntame: hana-11-virtual
SID: HSR
Instance ID: 77
hdb-11-85static:~ # su -l <sid>adm

hsradm@hdb-11-85static:/usr/sap/HSR/HDB77> hdbnsutil -listServices --allDatabases --sapcontrol=1 | grep -P "service_name|process_id|volume|database_name" | awk '/^service.*service_name=/ {s=$0; next} /^service.*process_id=/ {p=$0; next} /^service.*volume=/ {split($0,v,"="); if (v[2] != 0) {print s; print p; print $0; show_dbname=1} else {show_dbname=0}; next} /^service.*database_name=/ {if (show_dbname) {print $0; print ""}}'; for i in $(find -L /hana/log/${DIR_INSTANCE:9:3} -name 'hdb*' -type d) ; do echo $i ; hdblogdiag seglist $i | grep -oP 'Formatting|Preallocated|Writing|\bClosed\b|Truncated|BackedUp|RetainedFree|\bFree\b|Unknown|ClosedIncomplete|FreeIncomplete' | sort | uniq -c ; done

OUTPUT:

service/hana-11-virtual/37701/service_name=nameserver
service/hana-11-virtual/37701/process_id=7329
service/hana-11-virtual/37701/volume=1
service/hana-11-virtual/37701/database_name=

service/hana-11-virtual/37703/service_name=indexserver
service/hana-11-virtual/37703/process_id=26467
service/hana-11-virtual/37703/volume=3
service/hana-11-virtual/37703/database_name=HSR

service/hana-11-virtual/37707/service_name=xsengine
service/hana-11-virtual/37707/process_id=26470
service/hana-11-virtual/37707/volume=2
service/hana-11-virtual/37707/database_name=HSR

/hana/log/HSR/mnt00001/hdb00001
     21 Free
      1 Writing

/hana/log/HSR/mnt00001/hdb00003.00003
     20 Free
      1 Writing

/hana/log/HSR/mnt00001/hdb00002.00003
     22 Free
      1 RetainedFree
      1 Writing

Most of the log segments are in state Free and RetainedFree can removed via below commands:

Free
-> This is an indicator for a similar log volume full situation in the past. Although the log volume appears to be full, the free & already allocated (e.g. indexserver) log segments will be re-used.   
-> You can release the space occupied by the allocated log-segments in your file system:
#1: via SQL command: ALTER SYSTEM RECLAIM LOG;

#2: via OS command: hdbcons –p <PID_of_service> 'log release'

Via the results of the OS command in the very first step, Option B, you get back information about the PID (Process ID) and the Volume.

The Volume ID hides within the Information of "hdb0000x", x stands for the Volume ID.
The Volume Path and the PID can be used now to run the specific release command:

SAMPLE:
hsradm@hdb-11-85static:/usr/sap/HSR/HDB77> hdbcons -p 7329 'log release'

SAP HANA DB Management Client Console (type '\?' to get help for client commands)
Try to open connection to server process with PID 7329
SAP HANA DB Management Server Console (type 'help' to get help for server commands)
Executable: hdbnameserver (PID: 7329)

Service: nameserver@hana-11-virtual:37701 (DB#1: SYSTEMDB)
[OK]
--
## Start command at: 2025-05-26 11:02:15.249
Released 20 segments
[OK]
## Finish command at: 2025-05-26 11:02:15.255 command took: 5.589 msec
--
[EXIT]
--
[BYE]

 
RetainedFree

-> If the connection to the secondary is not available, the primary system will keep writing the redo log segments in the online log area to be prepared for the delta log shipping after the connection is reestablished.   

-> These log segments are marked as RetainedFree until the secondary is in sync again. , see KBA 2821539 - Volume /hana/log is full with log segments in status RetainedFree   

-> You can release the space occupied by the allocated log-segments in your file system via hdbcons by executing the OS Command as <sid>adm user: 

-> hdbcons –p <PID_of_service> 'log release -r' 

-> After deleting Log Segments "RetainedFree" the secondary will perform a full datashipping and log this message "Missing logsegments unavailable on primary - reinitialize"  

-> Check if the Parameter global.in/[system_replication]/enable_log_retention is set to force_on_takeover or force.

-> If yes then see 3102080 - RetainedFree status log segment accumulated on replication tier site while replication status is normal 

 

Configuration parameter related:   
logshipping_max_retention_size (KBA 2526877 - SAP HANA System Replication Parameter logshipping_max_retention_size) 

Log Retention and Multitarget System Replication:
SAP HANA System Replication 
SAP HANA Administration Guide for SAP HANA Platform 

 

How to Avoid Log Full Situations

To avoid log volumes running full due to non-working system replication follow the recommendations from SAP Guide:

SAP HANA Troubleshooting and Performance Analysis Guide - LogReplay: Managing the Size of the Log File at: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/bed8c14f9f024763b0777aa72b5436f6/d04835a010d3485c87992619dce89e2d.html?locale=en-US

 

1) Unregister an Unused Secondary

If the secondary is disconnected for a prolonged period and if it is not to be used as a replication server anymore, then disable / unregister and decouple the secondary site and disable the primary functionality.

This will stop the RetainedFree log entries from being written.

Unregister the secondary; this is normally done from the secondary site but can be done from the primary if the secondary is not available anymore,

SAP Note 1945676 - Correct usage of hdbnsutil -sr_unregister.

hdbnsutil –sr_unregister

Disable the primary (from the primary site. In a 3 way replication Site B (secondary) is the "primary" for SITE C):

hdbnsutil –sr_disable

Execute reconfigure on the primary site:

hdbnsutil –reconfig

You can use this same procedure for a primary which, after a takeover, will no longer be used for failback.

2) Set a Maximum Retention Size

Another option to manage the log size is to set a value for the logshipping_max_retention_size parameter. If the log size reaches this limit, then RetainedFree log entries will be overwritten. Note the following points in this case:

 

  • If any RetainedFree log entries are lost, then synchronization by logreplay will no longer be possible and a full data shipment will be necessary to resynchronize the secondary.
  • It is not possible to switch back to delta mode to resynchronize - only a full data shipping is possible.

Note that the parameter logshipping_max_retention_size is set for a service. See for the details SAP KBA 0002526877 - SAP HANA System Replication Parameter logshipping_max_retention_size.

3) Placeholder File

As a general precaution, to prevent any disk full situation from arising you can reserve a portion of the disk with an emergency placeholder file (containing any dummy values), for example, occupying 5 – 10 % of the file system. This file can then be deleted if ever necessary to quickly solve disk full situations.

4) Using the HANACleaner to automatically remove LogSegments with Status FREE, ##2399996 - How-To: Configuring automatic SAP HANA Cleanup with SAP HANACleaner

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/hana-housekeeping-using-hanacleaner/ba-p/13351394

 

Log Segment Knowledge, Background Information & Sources
Data and Log Volumes Documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/be3e5310bb571014b3fbd51035bc2383.html?locale=en-US&version=2.0.02 

  

The following log segment states exist:  

FormattingThe log segment is being formatted and not yet used.
PreallocatedThe log segment has been preallocated, but never used.
WritingThe log segment is currently being written.  
ClosedThe log segment is closed, not backed up and is still required for restart.  
TruncatedThe log segment is not required for restart, but has not been backed up.  
BackedUpSegment is already backed up, but a savepoint has not yet been written. Therefore it needs to be kept for instance recovery. 
RetainedFreeThe log segment has been backed up and is not required for restart, but is required to resync the system replication sites.  
FreeThe log segment has been backed up, it is not required for restart and can be reused.  

  

Per default each service can store up to 10240 log segments. 
If this number is reached, the log reports a log full error even if there is still disk space available.  
This limit can be changed: 2072410 - Enlarge limitation of log segment number of LogSegment Directory 
The new value of log segments that can be allocated can be checked via SQL (only) by connecting to each individual service: SELECT HOST,PORT,VOLUME_ID,PARTITION_ID,PATH,TOTAL_SEGMENTS FROM M_LOG_PARTITIONS  

  

SAP NOTES / KBAs Log related:  
Temporary Workaround: 1679938 - DiskFullEvent on Log Volume
2409471 - SAP HANA Log Volume Full on Secondary or Tertiary Site with System Replication 
2821539 - Volume /hana/log is full with log segments in status RetainedFree 
2781933 - Nameserver Crash at DataRecovery::LogSegment::freeRetainedSegment When Executing Command hdbcons log release -r
2988616 - /hana/shared volume full or growing
3047379 - Monitoring hana/log volume consumption
3142505 - Limit the Maximum Size Used by HANA Log Segments
SAMPLE: ALTER SYSTEM ALTER CONFIGURATION ('global.ini', 'SYSTEM' ) SET ('persistence', 'log_disk_usage_limit') = '85' WITH RECONFIGURE COMMENT SAP Note 3142505. Log volume usage size limited to 85%';  

2083715 - Analyzing log volume full situations
1679938 - DiskFullEvent on Log Volume
3537839 - SAP HANA Database Startup Issue Due to Insufficient Disk Space

entry.title: supplier invoice
entry.content:
On using loop condition as suggested by Google Gemini to prepare the code for BAdI FIN_ACDOC_ITEM_SUBSTITUTION for performing profit center substitution for intercompany supplier invoice journal entries as requested by our client, we are unable to get it to work as it reads such journal entries line by line. Due to the restrictions of this BAdI and the cloud environment, it is not working as expected. SAP has confirmed this restriction of the BAdI that when it operates in context of one item, the other items of posting can't be accessed.

We are getting a ODATA runtime error: 'TSV_TNEW_PAGE_ALLOC_FAILED' while using the loop condition. We are also getting similar runtime errors while performing other functions like PGI for outbound delivery, MIGO post goods receipt etc.

 

Also conditions like class and method can't be used for coding due to non availability of these keywords in the cloud environment

entry.title: Authorization group
entry.content:
How They Work

When a user attempts to access an SAP object, the system checks two things:

1️⃣Whether the user has the necessary authorization (through roles and profiles)
2️⃣Whether the user is authorized for the specific authorization group assigned to that object

If an object belongs to an authorization group that the user cannot access, the system will deny access even if the user has other relevant permissions.

Common Use Cases

Table Protection: Database tables containing sensitive information (like payroll data, financial records, or personal information) are assigned to specific authorization groups. Only users with appropriate clearance can access these tables through transactions like SE16 or development tools.

Program Security: Custom programs or reports can be assigned authorization groups to ensure only designated users can execute them, particularly useful for programs that manipulate critical business data.

Configuration and Assignment

Authorization groups are typically defined in customization tables and then assigned to objects through various methods depending on the object type. Users are granted access to authorization groups through their roles, which contain authorization objects (like S_TABU_DIS for table access) that specify which authorization groups they can access.

Demo

Create new authorization group in the SM30, view name V_TPGP.

Add this group to the program (see explanation above). Now if the user without appropriate clearance try to execute the report the error message will be appears.

Add the group to the related field of the object.

The role should be assigned to the user.

Now the program can be executed

entry.title: Enhanced Organizational Structure Profile in SAP Readiness Check for SAP ERP Usage & Data Profiling
entry.content:
Organizations planning a transition to SAP S/4HANA require comprehensive tools that offer insights into their existing SAP ERP Central Component (SAP ECC) systems. The Organizational Structure Profile within SAP Readiness Check for SAP ERP Usage and Data Profiling serves this purpose. Today, we proudly announce the availability of the new version of the Organizational Structure Profile as part of SAP Readiness Check for SAP ERP Usage and Data Profiling. We deliver significantly enhanced analysis features in this latest version, providing better clarity, deeper analytical capabilities, and a more user-friendly interface.

Figure 1: Updated Version of the Organizational Structure Profile

Understanding the Business Context

The Organizational Structure Profile provides a holistic overview of an organization's SAP ECC 6.0 system structure. This profile is essential for understanding the relationships between various organizational units and identifying opportunities for optimization before transitioning to SAP S/4HANA. The Organizational Structure Profile automates a previously manual and resource-intensive process, enabling organizations to reduce the time and cost associated with scoping and assessing organizational objects.

The enhancements in this new version address several shortcomings of the previous version, including:

  • Limited scope: The previous version lacked coverage for certain organizational items (e.., controlling areas, storage locations, distribution channels, etc.), preventing a comprehensive analysis.
  • Lack of relationship data: The absence of explicit relationship data hindered a complete understanding of organizational dynamics.
  • Simplistic user interface: Users were unable to efficiently access and interact with detailed and interconnected information due to a flat and feature-limited user interface.

The latest version addresses these shortcomings, transforming the Organizational Structure Profile into a more robust and insightful tool.

Key Capabilities of the Enhanced Organizational Structure Profile

Expanded Scope and Coverage of Organizational Item Types

The updated version significantly broadens its scope by including additional organizational item types essential for a comprehensive understanding of the organizational structure. These include:

  • Controlling Areas
  • Cost Centers
  • Profit Centers
  • Company Codes
  • Plants
  • Shipping Points
  • Storage Locations
  • Purchasing Organizations
  • Sales Organizations
  • Distribution Channels
  • Divisions

This expanded coverage gives organizations a holistic view of their structure, ensuring that all relevant organizational elements are considered and more thoroughly examined during the transition. This insight allows project teams to make informed decisions during the transition planning phase.

Enhanced Visualization of Organizational Relationships

This new version offers improved visualization capabilities to clarify the complex relationships between organizational items. These include:

  • Hierarchical network graphs: Graphical representations display parent (ancestor) and child relationships, clearly visualizing organizational hierarchies.
  • Color-coded structures: Different organizational types are represented with distinct colors, improving the clarity and usability of network visualizations.
  • Expandable/collapsible elements: Network components can expand and collapse, allowing users to focus on specific areas of interest within the organizational structure.

Figure 2: The Parent Object and Child Object Network Graphs of a Sales Organization Item

These advanced visualization capabilities facilitate a more intuitive understanding of organizational structures and interdependencies, empowering project teams to make well-informed decisions during transition planning.

Detailed Usage Analysis for Making Informed Decisions

The enhancements include incorporating detailed usage analysis to help organizations quickly identify actively used organizational items and those that may be obsolete. Historically, determining whether an organizational unit is actively contributing to business processes or is no longer relevant is time-consuming. This new version now simplifies this process with advanced usage indicators, enabling teams to streamline assessments, accelerate decision-making based on usage data, and optimize resources by eliminating obsolete elements.

To evaluate the usage status of an organizational item, related business objects for all organizational item types in scope were identified and are used to analyze their data volumes and activities regarding the selected organization. The resulting data for these business objects are presented in the "Usage Indicator" card of the detail view (see figure 3). Each business object is assigned a usage status, categorized as "Active," "Likely Not Active," or "Not Active":

  • Active (Green): Indicates that the business object is currently actively used for the organizational item in focus, having a data volume higher than 20 items and recent activity over the last three years.
  • Likely Not Active (Yellow): States that the usage status of this business object is uncertain about the selected organizational item, with limited data volumes or lack of recent activity, warranting further investigation.
  • Not Active (Red): Confirms that the business object is not being used for the selected organizational item, with a data volume less than 20 items and no recent activity over the last three years.

Based on the usage indicators of the related business objects, the usage status of the selected organizational item is determined and presented in the header of the detail view (see figure 3):

  • Active (Green): The organizational item is currently actively used. It has at least one related business object which is actively used.
  • Likely Not Active (Yellow): The usage status of the selected organizational item is ambiguous, demanding further investigation. No related business object is actively used, but there are objects with usage status "Likely Not Active" and/or "Not Active."
  • Not Active (Red): The organizational item is not being used. All related business objects have the usage indicator value "Not Active."

For a more detailed understanding of calculating usage indicators, click on the 'Learn More' button to access the Organizational Structure Profile help panel.

Figure 3: Detail View of a Plant Organizational Item, Including Usage Indicators Card and Open Help Panel

We strongly recommend that the project team, particularly the business owners of the involved organizational units, conduct further investigations to validate and confirm the findings from the usage data analysis. This additional validation ensures the accuracy and reliability of the insights, facilitating more informed decision-making.

Identification of Orphaned Organizational Items

The enhanced version automatically identifies, and flags orphaned organizational items that lack proper parent-child hierarchy links, signaling potential structural inefficiencies or errors. By highlighting these gaps, the Organizational Structure Profile enables organizations to address structural inconsistencies proactively, minimize transition risks, and ensure the integrity and completeness of their organizational model throughout the transition process.

Figure 4: A Plant Without a Corresponding Company Code is Marked as an Orphaned Item

 

Redesigned User Interface for Enhanced Usability

To address usability concerns, the enhanced Organizational Structure Profile features a completely redesigned user interface that significantly improves usability:

  • Flexible column layout: The interface is divided into two main sections - a list of organizational items on the left and detailed information about the selected item on the right.
  • Intuitive filtering: Users can filter organizational items by type through a view selector or search for specific items using a text-based search field.
  • Detailed information sections: The detail view includes:
    • Master data information
    • Usage indicators showing the status of related business objects
    • Dependent data sections displaying related organizational items
    • Network graphs visualizing parent and child hierarchies
  • Export capabilities: Users can export both the list of organizational items and detailed information about specific items for further analysis or reporting.
  • Related data access: Links to related data collectors enable users to access additional information relevant to the selected organizational item type.

Figure 5: Detail View of a Company Code Organizational Item and Available Filter Options

This newly designed user interface makes it easier for project teams to navigate and analyze complex organizational structures without requiring specialized knowledge.

Cross-Profile Integration

The enhanced Organizational Structure Profile integrates seamlessly with other profiles within the SAP Readiness Check for SAP ERP Usage and Data Profiling:

  • Usage indicators are linked to the Initial Data Migration Profile, allowing users to navigate directly to detailed information about related business objects. This integration provides a more holistic view of the system landscape and facilitates comprehensive analysis.
  • Hyperlinks to related data collectors enable users to access additional information and gain a more comprehensive understanding of their system landscape.

Figure 6: A Company Code Organizational Item and its Collection of Links to Related Data Collectors

Use Cases

Transitioning to SAP S/4HANA can be more than just a data migration exercise - it could be a strategic opportunity to optimize business structures and eliminate inefficiencies. The enhanced Organizational Structure Profile is a powerful tool that offers valuable insights regardless of the transition scenario.

Selective Data Transition Planning

Understanding the organizational structure and usage patterns is crucial for projects involving selective data transition. The new version helps identify actively used organizational elements, highlighting those that should be migrated versus those that can be discarded, optimizing the transition process.

Organizational Structure Optimization

Many organizations use the transition to SAP S/4HANA as an opportunity to streamline their organizational structure. The enhanced analysis provides the insight needed to identify redundant or unused organizational elements, supporting informed decision-making during this optimization process.

Transition Sequence Planning

Understanding dependencies between organizational items is essential for planning the transition sequence. The network visualization capabilities of the Organizational Structure Profile make these dependencies clear, helping project teams develop more effective transition strategies.

Data Quality Assessment

Orphaned item detection and usage analysis contribute to an overall data quality assessment. This evaluation enables organizations to address inconsistencies and ensure that only relevant, high-quality data migrates to the new system.

Next Steps for Users

Follow these steps to take advantage of the new Organizational Structure Profile:

  1. System preparation and data collection: Implement the latest version of SAP Note 3112362 and run an SAP Readiness Check for SAP ERP Usage and Data Profiling analysis by following the instructions described within the SAP Note, including uploading the data collection archive to the SAP Readiness Check cloud application.
  2. Explore the analysis results: Identify organizational items of interest and examine their usage and dependent data.
  3. Visualize hierarchical relationships: Use the network graphs to understand the relationships between specific organizational items.
  4. Filter and search: Use the filtering and search capabilities to focus on specific segments of the organizational structure.
  5. Identify optimization opportunities: Review usage status indicators to identify potentially unused or redundant organizational items.
  6. Investigate orphaned items: Address organizational items with disrupted hierarchy paths to ensure structural consistency.
  7. Explore related data: Use the links to associated data collectors to gain additional insights about specific organizational item types.
  8. Export for collaboration: Export relevant data for sharing with stakeholders and for further analysis.

As an alternative to creating a new analysis in the SAP Readiness Check cloud application, you can update your existing SAP Readiness Check for SAP ERP Usage and Data Profiling analysis. Start by implementing the latest version of SAP Note 3112362 and rerun the data collection process in your SAP ECC system. Then, using the newly created data collection archive, update the existing analysis data using the Update Analysis option within the header of the dashboard view in the SAP Readiness Check cloud application on SAP for Me or your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.

 As always, we look forward to receiving your feedback.

SAP Readiness Check team

entry.title: Join the SAP S/4HANA Service, Private Cloud Edition Early Adopter Care Program
entry.content:
Are you running classic Customer Service (CS) within the Compatibility Scope in S/4HANA*, and wondering how to transition to SAP S/4HANA Service?

You’re not alone. Many organizations are asking the same questions: Where do we start? What’s the benefit? Who can guide us?

We understand the challenges, and we’re here to support your transformation journey.

* Note: The Compatibility Scope for CS is set to expire at the end of 2030 (unlike most cases, which expire at the end of 2025). This makes it essential to begin planning your transition. For more details, refer to SAP Note 2269324 and this blog post.

Why join this program?

The SAP S/4HANA Service, Private Cloud Edition Early Adopter Care Program is your opportunity to engage directly with SAP’s product development teams. Our mission is simple: to refine and evolve our solutions based on real customer needs. Your voice matters.

As a participant, you’ll gain direct access to SAP product experts who can guide you through your transition from CS to S/4HANA Service. You’ll receive support with product-related questions and benefit from close monitoring of your critical issues. Most importantly, you’ll have the opportunity to share your feedback and help shape the future of SAP Service – at no cost to you.

Who should join?

This program is designed for customers who are licensed for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and are running on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS0 or higher. If you're still using classic CS in the Compatibility Scope within SAP S/4HANA and have concrete plans for a proof of concept or implementation project, this is your chance to get expert guidance. We’re looking for participants who are ready to engage regularly, share their experiences, and become reference customers.

To make the most of the program, you will need a dedicated project team and a well-defined internal setup to drive the implementation.

How to apply

Apply now via the SAP Influence Portal and become part of a select group helping shape the future of SAP service management capabilities. Let’s build the future of SAP S/4HANA Service together.

Join the SAP S/4HANA Service, Private Cloud Edition Early Adopter Care Program and take the next step in your transformation journey.

If you have any questions, feel free to contact us: SAP_EAC_S4H_Service@sap.com.

Download a copy of the one-pager (image below).

Learn more about SAP S/4HANA Service

  • SAP S/4HANA Service | SAP Community – collection of webinars, blog articles, links to certification program and other materials. It’s a great entry point to see all resources on SAP S/4HANA Service.
  • Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Service – 30-min webinar I conducted. I show some concepts in diagrams and show system demonstrations of highlights in the 2023 release (FPS0) for SAP S/4HANA Service.

entry.title: Re: Facing an issue while executing the Actions Artifacts...
entry.content:
Hello,

Due to some missing information, I can unfortunately only guess what went wrong when posting budget data via API API_FINPLANNINGDATA.

Posting budget data to ACDOCP is subject to additional requirements. For example project budget (planning category BUDGET01)  may only be uploaded as a whole and must therefore include the budget for the entire project. As a consequence, the delete scope must NOT be set for PS_POSID (Work Breakdown Structure Element), POPER/FISCPER and RBUKRS (Company Code), otherwise only a partial update of the project budget would be made (that is not permitted).

Similar limitations apply for cost center budget (planning category BUDGET02) that may only be uploaded as a whole and must therefore include the budget for the entire year. In order to ensure a complete cost center budget, the delete scope must NOT be set for the FISCPER or POPER.

Can you please revise your payload according to the above information and try again to post budget data?

If the problems still occurs, for further analyses I need the payload and the complete response from your post request.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Processes can now be scheduled 📅!
entry.content:
First, when you say configured in the same agent, that means you are talking of automations, not processes. Can you clarify?

As for processes, a specific process cannot be both scheduled and triggered by API. You could have a process triggered in API and then create another process that wraps the first that is scheduled.

When the process gets triggered by API or scheduler, that creates a process instance and that instance cannot be triggered/retriggered (only processes get triggered, creating a process instance). That instance will run and will not affect any other instances (except as they update the same backend).

As for a process that has automation, when the automation is reached in the process, it will be "triggered" and run, assuming the agent tenant/mode is set properly.

P.S.: I'm hope this helped, but I feel I may not have answered your questions. Please clarify the questions if you need additional help.

entry.title: SuccessFactors - Variable Pay Basis Calculation
entry.content:
Hi All,

Good day,
I'm trying to look for the system feasibility regarding the requirement I received from my customer. You may refer to the pictures to make two scenarios below more visualize

Business considered 1st of January to 31st December 20XX as their bonus fiscal/calendar year

Scenario 1 is basically a scenario in which the employee does not get a promotion and that's why the bonus calculation should only consider the salary based on the merit Review in April (a specific as of date), excluding the salary from 1st January until March (1000 USD)

But once an employee receives a promotion scenario 2 shall take place. What does this mean:

In case of promotion the bonus calculation should consider the salary (1100 USD) based of Merit Review (in April) and the salary (1300 USD) based of Promotion (in August), excluding the salary from 1st January until March (1000 USD)

entry.title: Part 2 Webinar Entitlement Mgmt: Preview for Scope Item “5SW – Subscription Management for Software"
entry.content:
Join us for this webinar on SAP Entitlement Management, offering Part 2 of the deep dive preview of the planned Scope Item "5SW – Subscription Management for Software" on Tuesday, June 24, 2025, at 4:00 PM CEST / 10:00 AM EST. 

Register now!

This one-hour session is Part 2 of the webinars on scope item 5SW, starting with a short recap of the content of the first webinar held on May 13, 2024, which you can review here,

Part 2 of the webinar will explain and show demos for various subscription change processes which are planned to be supported by the scope item, as well as automated application, tenant and capability decommissioning. 

In addition, we will also have a look into key elements of the system setup for the scope item, for which a detailed setup guide and end to end test script is planned to be made available with the 2508 release. 

We will also give an outlook on the product direction and product vision for "Subscription Management for Software", with SAP Business Data Cloud leveraging license consumption, software usage and adoption data as the basis for customer success, using Analytics Cloud stories, AI Agents, or integration to Gainsight Customer Success. 

Register now and review the detailed agenda below!

More information on EMS - see my Summary Blog on SAP Entitlement Management 

Customer Webinar Part 2 – SAP Entitlement Management:
Deep Dive Preview for Scope Item “5SW – Subscription Management for Software”

Date: Tuesday, June 24, 2025
Time: 4:00 PM Central European Summer Time / 10:00 AM EST
Duration: 1 hour

Agenda:

  • Introduction & Overview of Scope Item 5SW
    • Purpose & business value of Subscription Management for Software
    • Key capabilities: End-to-end integration and automation from sales to fulfillment/provisioning, billing, and revenue recognition
  • Live Demo: Subscription Management in Action – Subscription Change Processes, System Setup for Automated Provisioning, Business Data Cloud and AI Providing Insights for Customer Success Processes
    • Summary of demo flow in first session on May 13, 2025 (review here)
    • Demo of different change processes, e.g.

entry.title: Re: PCR for making Gratuity calculation as Zero for selec...
entry.content:
Do a Payroll Simulation for an employee of sub-group AG, with the "Display Log" box checked, and then drill-down in that log to find out where WTs 1216 and 9423 are generated, calculated, and then exported to the Results Table.

Before these WTs are exported to table RT, create a PCR that will read these 2 specific WTs.  For each of those WTs, make a decision on the employee sub-group (OUTWPPERSB).  If sub-group AG, use operation ZERO= RNA and nothing else, but if any other sub-group use operation ADDWT *. 

Note that you may require 2 PCRs if WTs 1216 and 9423 do not exist in the same sub-schema.

P.S. If WTs 1216 and 9423 are used to calculate something else that is required for ESG "AG", you will have to insert the PCR after that extra calculation.

entry.title: New Integration Content for Coupa to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence Now Available
entry.content:
We’re excited to announce that new integration content for Coupa to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence is now available, offering organizations an enhanced capability to analyze their end-to-end procurement processes. This valuable integration is accessible free of charge on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, empowering businesses to make data-driven decisions with greater precision and efficiency.

Streamline Your Procurement Analysis

Procurement processes are complex, involving numerous stakeholders and steps that can create inefficiencies and gaps if not closely monitored. By leveraging the new integration content for Coupa and SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, you can gain comprehensive insights into your procurement workflows. This integration helps you visualize, analyze, and optimize the entire procurement process from purchase requisition to payment, ensuring greater transparency and continuous improvement.

How to Access the Integration Content

Accessing this powerful integration is straightforward. To get started:

Log in to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

 

Navigate to the “Explore” tab and search for “SAP Signavio”.

 

Locate the new integration content for Coupa.

 

View the integration package and dedicated integration flows.

 

The integration package includes dedicated integration flows that outline the relevant business objects and events extracted by default. This setup enables a quick start with the flexibility to adjust and extend the content to meet your specific business needs.

Key Benefits of the Integration

  • End-to-End Visibility: Gain a holistic view of your procurement operations, identifying bottlenecks and opportunities for optimization.

  • Customizable Data Extraction: The predefined business objects and events can be tailored to your organization’s unique requirements, offering adaptability for varied use cases.

  • Enhanced Decision-Making: With seamless data extraction from Coupa and integration into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, organizations can make informed decisions based on accurate process execution data.

Share Your Feedback

We value your input and look forward to hearing your thoughts on this new integration package. Share your experiences and suggestions in the comments section below.

Start analyzing and optimizing your procurement processes today with the new Coupa to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence integration. Discover how this solution can transform your operations and drive better outcomes.

In case you have not seen it yet, also check out the integration content we have recently released for Salesforce (Blog post / Integration content) and Microsoft Dynamics CRM (Blog post / Integration content)

If you are in SAP S4 HANA Public cloud, it comes with pre defined Operating chart of Accounts YCOA and it also comes with pre defined GL accounts with account determination settings. You can create new GL account for any additional requirement but all basic GL accounts are already pre defined in the SAP S4 HANA Public cloud.

entry.title: Re: Synchronise Tasks from Jira to SAP CALM
entry.content:
Hi Quteiba,

You've probably already seen documentation about the External API Management app. The direction of this communication is SAP Cloud ALM -> third party service. We offer generic guides and documentation for integrating the API of a third party service with your SAP Cloud ALM tenant. The flow of events is:

  1. Some kind of an event situation is created (e.g. a metric threshold is passed).
  2. SAP Cloud ALM makes a request to the API of the third party service.
  3. The third party service responds to the API request by performing an action (e.g. creating a task, ticket, etc.).
  4. The third party service sends a JSON payload back to SAP Cloud ALM to inform about the result about the API request and action.

This workflow is configurable in SAP Cloud ALM because the API requests are being created and executed on the SAP Cloud ALM side. 

Let's compare this to communication in the direction of third party service -> SAP Cloud ALM (e.g. Jira -> SAP Cloud ALM). In this scenario, the third party service is going to make API requests to the SAP Cloud ALM APIs. The flow of events is different:

  1. Some action is taken in the third party service (e.g. closing a Jira Task).
  2. The third party service creates and executes a request to one of the SAP Cloud ALM APIs.
  3. SAP Cloud ALM responds to the API request by performing an action (e.g. closing a Task in an SAP Cloud ALM Project).
  4. SAP Cloud ALM sends a JSON payload back to the third party service to inform about the result about the API request and action.

Because the API requests will be created and executed in the third party service, SAP Cloud ALM has no ability to configure or otherwise influence that process. SAP Cloud ALM can only respond and act on the API requests it receives from the third party service. Because of this, there are no guides owned by SAP Cloud ALM specifically for integrating communication in the direction of a third party service -> SAP Cloud ALM. 

However, we do offer extensive documentation for our various Push APIs. Third party services can make requests to these APIs, as long as the third party service supports the ability to execute API requests. We cannot offer specific guidance for configuring third party services to make API requests, but we can assist with errors and issues when our API is being called (e.g. the third party service makes the request, but SAP Cloud ALM responds in an unexpected way).

You mentioned that you want to create/update SAP Cloud ALM Project Tasks when you perform actions on Jira Tasks. I am not a Jira expert, so I cannot speak to its suite of functionality. If Jira does not support the execution of API requests, then you will not be able to configure the worklfow you desire. If Jira does support executing API requests though, you'll need to configure your Jira system to make requests using the SAP Cloud ALM Tasks API. You can create (POST), update (PATCH), and even delete (DELETE) SAP Cloud ALM project Tasks using that API. 

If you want to experiment with the Tasks API, you can check out the SAP Cloud ALM Tasks page on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. The Try Out page will allow you to perform some tests in a dummy environment, or configure a connection to your SAP Cloud ALM tenant and run actual API requests to manipulate SAP Cloud ALM Project Tasks. You may also need to experiment with the SAP Cloud ALM Project API in order to identify the correct Project data required as part of the Tasks API request JSON body.

Thanks and regards, 
John

entry.title: Re: Fixed Assets Migration - Asset Value Date and Depreci...
entry.content:
Hi,

If you are doing Asset Data migration during the fiscal year then "Transaction during FY" tab information in Migration file is important. You should use Transaction type 100 for Acquisition and put Value date in the file same as your legacy transfer date i.e. 30/04/2035. This should solve your issue.

Try this, it should work. Check and let me know.

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Returnable packaging owned by us to be used for vendor deliveries
entry.content:
We have a request to set up a process for keeping track of pallets that are in rotation between us and a supplier. The pallets will be owned by us and assumption is that they will managed as VERP materials with a valuation. We need to keep track of how many pallets are in our warehouse and how many are shipped out to the supplier. We will ship initial stock of pallets to the supplier, receive them back from them with their materials on them twice a week, and when the truck does the milk-run they will pick up some pallets and ship them to the supplier to refill the stock at their plant.

Since volume of pallets is low and deliveries only happens twice a week business is prepared to have a simple process with some manual intervention. But ideally we should post the incoming from our vendor automatically and for the outbound process have a delivery w/o reference and a PGI so we can print dispatch note and waybill for the delivery.

 

We have an inbound process where we create inbound delivery and post goods issue with 107 movement type to stock in transit when supplier send the ASN, and as second step we do a 109 movement into stock upon arrival to our plant. As part of creating the inbound delivery upon the ASN we are determining the packaging material from packaging instruction. Here we can include our owned pallet, but not sure how we can control the movement type?

 

Has anyone come across similar requirement and have a solution proposal including which movement types to use?

entry.title: Re: Handling Bank Charges During Incoming Payments in SAP...
entry.content:
Hi,

To book difference in incoming payment to bank charges , you need to do configuration in FBKP - Automatic postings->Bank transactions->Bank charges. Here you need to assign Bank charges GL account.

Once this configuration is done, while doing incoming payment, you can see the bank charges field, just put the bank charges amount in that field. It will automatically post the bank charges to configured GL account.

Example of entry

Bank Incoming account DR 90

Bank Charges account DR 10

              Customer Account CR 100

 

I hope this clears. Please check and confirm.

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Re: How to test RAP odata V4 custom action?
entry.content: Hi Neil! You're fine - 204 actually means success but without any returning data. Your way of calling it is correct - when we're testing manually, we need to add the namespace.action to our endpoint. Check your action signature on the behaviour definition, you can refer to the input/output parameters here: https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/action-definition?q=actions

entry.title: Re: SAC "Only data of the table that are on the current p...

Here are the steps to create excel export button

  1. Create script variable

Use left side panel as shown below

 

 

 

 

  1. Select Include widget option and select the table you want to export

 

 

 

  1. Add Button

Write below script on button

 

Application.showBusyIndicator();

 

ExportToExcel_1.exportReport();

 

Application.hideBusyIndicator();

 

Hope this will work for you.

Thanks.

entry.title: Introducing the Get Certified Series for SAP Certified Professional - Developer - SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Are you aiming to become a certified SAP Commerce Cloud developer? Do you want to deepen your understanding of the platform and confidently prepare for the P_C4H34 certification exam? We’re excited to announce a comprehensive Get Certified live session series designed specifically to support you on this journey!

Overview

This series

Get Certified: SAP Certified Professional - Developer - SAP Commerce Cloud

features expert-led knowledge transfer sessions conducted by certified trainers who will guide you through all the key topics necessary to succeed in the P_C4H34: SAP Certified Professional - Developer - SAP Commerce Cloud exam. The goal? To provide you with targeted, instructor-led support that complements your self-study efforts, helping you build confidence and mastery of the exam content.

For registration, simply log in with your valid Learning Hub subscription (for SAP internal teams, it’s FREE) and register to reserve your spot.

Why Attend?

Preparing for certification involves more than just studying on your own. Our live sessions offer:

  • Expert guidance: Learn directly from experienced trainers who understand what’s required to pass.
  • Focused walkthroughs: Dive deep into critical topics and exam question types.
  • Resources and references: Obtain valuable materials to extend your learning after each session.
  • Q&A opportunities: Clarify doubts and get tips for the exam.

Please note, this series is different from the previous live session recording, "Certification Live Session: SAP Commerce Cloud - Developer Certification Preparation" While that session provided a helpful overview of the essential exam topics, it did not delve into each area with detailed explanations, sample questions, or comprehensive coverage.

Our upcoming Get Certified series offers a deeper, more focused approach, with expert-guided walkthroughs of key topics, practical tips, and sample questions designed to maximize your exam readiness.

How to Prepare

Start your certification journey by reviewing the Trainings or Learning Journeys associated with SAP Commerce Cloud listed at the end of this blog post. These will give you a road map of what to learn. Then, participate in our live sessions to receive a structured, guided review of exam content, focusing on the most important areas for success.

Session Breakdown

Here's what we have planned:

Session 1: Kick-Off (1 hour)

  • Introduction to the series and certification
  • Explanation of exam question types and tips on preparation
  • Walkthrough of sample questions using the exam interface

Session 2: Exploring Platform Basics and Platform Features (1.5 hours)

  • Review of core platform concepts
  • Understanding the system architecture and key functionalities essential for the exam

Session 3: Mastering Product Content Management & Commerce (1 hour)

  • Insights into managing product data
  • Managing commerce operations within SAP Commerce Cloud

Session 4: Navigating Customer Experience, Order Management, Customer Services, and Backoffice Framework (1.5 hours)

  • Deep dive into the web content management module (WCMS)
  • Managing orders, customer services, and backoffice functionalities

Session 5: Understanding Commerce Cloud and Cloud Portal, Integration Support and Managing Clean Core (1 hour)

  • Overview of SAP Commerce Cloud (in the public Cloud) and Cloud Portal UI
  • Overview for the integration support of SAP Commerce Cloud
  • How to manage the Clean Core principal in SAP

Learning Objectives

By participating in this series, you will:

  • Gain a clear understanding of the exam structure and scope
  • Cover the essential topic areas required for certification success
  • Build confidence to attempt the P_C4H34 exam with expert guidance and practice

Relevant Learning Journeys

To maximize your preparation(best before attending the get certified series), review the following Trainings or Learning Journeys:

  • Managing Clean Core for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and 
  • either:
    • SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 1
    • SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 2
  • or:
    • Exploring the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud
    • Expanding upon Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud
    • Mastering the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud

Ready to take your SAP Commerce Cloud skills to the next level and achieve certification? Don’t miss this opportunity to learn from the best, clarify your doubts, and prepare effectively for the P_C4H34 exam. 

See you in July or October! Let’s get certified together!

entry.title: Re: Add field CustomerAccountAssignmentGroup to API/BO In...
entry.content:
In sales order OData Api, it has field ‘CustomerAccountAssignmentGroup’  ,  in returns order OData API, such field is not exposed.

You can only maintain this value via the UI/App after returns order is created via API.

If you need this field in the API , you can raise a CIP request for this field.

entry.title: Re: Assistance Required – Agent-on-Fly Configuration for...
entry.content: Hi @Vera_Jaing, We are on ECC6 EHP7 with Windows Server 2019 and MS-SQL database environment with Always-On High Availability (HA) enabled. C:\Program Files\SAP\hostctrl\exe\saphostctrl.exe -function GetComputerSystem. On Windows, we have run the above command on the two virtual servers ineccprdci, ineccprddb and attached the outputs and screenshots of diagnostics agent and configuration for your analysis: Hostname FQDN IP address Cluster IP address Host Type ineccprdci ineccprdci.xyz.com 172.X.X.151 192.X.X.151 Virtual (MS-SQL Always-On, High Availability windows failover cluster & SAP Central Instance) ineccprddb ineccprddb.xyz.com 172.X.X.150 192.X.X.150 Virtual (MS-SQL Always-On, High Availability windows failover cluster & SAP Application) ineccsapgrp ineccsapgrp.xyz.com 172.X.X.154 Logical (SAP Message Server Group) ineccdbgrp,1433 ineccdbgrp.xyz.com 172.X.X.153 Logical (Database Listener with Port) infsserver infsserver.xyz.com 172.X.X.152 Logical (Shared Filesystem for SAP HA) ineccapp2–9 ineccapp[2–9].xyz.com 172.X.X.[155-165] Virtual (Application Instances) The expected logical host are listed in the command result and The logical hosts have their own IP addresses mappings. All the IP address with hostnames are mapped in Windows Central DNS server and no local system maintenance of the etc/hosts file. The two virtual servers ineccprdci, ineccprddb shows the Cluster/heartbeat IP address (192.X.X.151, 192.X.X.150) in SAP Diagnostics Agent and same values are replicated in LMDB for them, instead of actual/real IP address (172.X.X.151, 172.X.X.150). Due to Solution Manager unable to reach the Cluster/Heartbeat IP address (192.X.X.151, 192.X.X.150) and metric/alerts triggers host is not available. I have manually set the hostnames with actual/real IP address (172.X.X.151, 172.X.X.150) in Application Configuration Tab at SAP Diagnostics Agent Administration and updated the actual/real IP address (172.X.X.151, 172.X.X.150) manually in LMDB also for two virtual servers ineccprdci, ineccprddb. The Agent-on-Fly functionality has also been activated on both hosts. However, we are encountering the following issues: On ineccprdci, only the logical hostname ineccsapgrp.xyz.com is available for creating the Agent-on-Fly Diagnostics Agent. The logical hostname ineccdbgrp.xyz.com:1433 (used for the database listener) is not listed. On ineccprddb, neither ineccsapgrp.xyz.com nor ineccdbgrp.xyz.com:1433 appear as options for Agent-on-Fly Diagnostics Agent creation. Could you kindly advise on the correct procedure to register and configure the Agent-on-Fly Diagnostics Agent for the logical host ineccdbgrp.xyz.com:1433 on either ineccprdci or ineccprddb? Regards Srinivas V
